title: Adding & subtracting decimals word problems
description: Practice solving word problems by adding or subtracting decimal numbers.
topic_path: Module 2: Arithmetic operations including dividing by a fraction
Topic B: Multi‐digit decimal operations—adding, subtracting, and multiplying

assessment_items.question_content:
To visit his grandmother, Luis takes a train $6.37$ kilometers and a car $5.45$ kilometers. 

 **How many kilometers is Luis's journey in total?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilometers
hints: To find the total distance Luis travels, we need to add the two distances together.
Distance on train + distance on car = total distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
To visit his grandmother, Michael takes a motorcycle $3.85$ kilometers and a horse $3.32$ kilometers. In total, the journey takes $50.54$ minutes. 

 **How many kilometers is Michael's journey in total?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilometers
hints: To find the total distance Michael travels, we need to add the two distances together.
Distance on motorcycle + distance on horse = total distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
To get to school each morning, Vanessa takes a horse $19.33$ kilometers and a train $2.27$ kilometers. In total, the journey takes $26.48$ minutes. 

 **How many kilometers is Vanessa's journey in total?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilometers
hints: To find the total distance Vanessa travels, we need to add the two distances together.
Distance on horse + distance on train = total distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
To get to work each morning, Emily takes a horse $10.98$ kilometers and a bike $4.06$ kilometers. 

 **How many kilometers is Emily's journey in total?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilometers
hints: To find the total distance Emily travels, we need to add the two distances together.
Distance on horse + distance on bike = total distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
To visit her grandmother, Jessica takes a horse $3.31$ kilometers and a motorcycle $1$ kilometer. 

 **How many kilometers is Jessica's journey in total?**

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilometers
hints: To find the total distance Jessica travels, we need to add the two distances together.
Distance on horse + distance on motorcycle = total distance.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sofia's batting average is $0.022$ higher than Joud's batting average. Joud has a batting average of $0.169$.

**What is Sofia's batting average?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Sofia's batting average $=$ Joud's batting average ${}+0.022$.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&\\
&0.&1&6&9\\
+&0.&0&2&2\\
\hline
&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
Sofia has a batting average of $0.191$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A candy bar is broken into two pieces. One piece is $33.641 \text{ mm}$ long, and the second piece is $31.749 \text{ mm}$ long. 

**How long was the whole candy bar before it broke?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{mm}$
hints: First candy piece $+$ second candy piece $=$ total distance

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&&\\
&3&3.&6&4&1\\
+&3&1.&7&4&9\\
\hline
&&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
The length of the whole candy bar was $65.39\,\text{mm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
According to Jax's pedometer, he walked $2.783\,\text{km}$ while taking care of the livestock in the morning and $3.124\,\text{km}$ taking care of the livestock in the evening.

**How far did Jax walk while taking care of the livestock today?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{km}$
hints: Distance in the morning $+$ distance in the evening $=$ total distance.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&\\
&2.&7&8&3\\
+&3.&1&2&4\\
\hline
&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
Jax walked $5.907\,\text{km}$ while taking care of the livestock today.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kylie has a modern quarter with a mass of $5.623\,\text{g}$ and an older silver quarter with a mass of $6.24\,\text{g}$.

**What is the combined mass of the quarters?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{g}$
hints: Mass of modern quarter $+$ mass of older quarter $=$ total mass.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&&\\
&&5.&6&2&3\\
+&&6.&2&4&0\\
\hline
&&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
The total mass of the quarters is $11.863\,\text{g}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Himari gave birth to twins and named them Adrian and Chang. When they were first born, Adrian weighed $2.87$ kilograms and was $54.5$ centimeters tall, and Chang weighed $4.54$ kilograms and was $51$ centimeters tall. 

 **How much did the babies weigh in total?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilograms
hints: Adrian's weight $+$ Chang's weight $=$ total weight.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&\\
&2.&8&7\\
+&4.&5&4\\
\hline
&.&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
The total weight of the babies is $7.41$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
To visit her grandmother, Melek takes a motorcycle $3.8$ kilometers and a horse $3.32$ kilometers. In total, the journey takes $50.54$ minutes. 

 **How many kilometers is Melek's journey in total?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilometers
hints: Distance on motorcycle $+$ distance on horse $=$ total distance.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&\\
&3.&8&0\\
+&3.&3&2\\
\hline
&.&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.

assessment_items.question_content:
Darragh has a Golden Eagle coin in his collection with a mass of $13.551\,\text{g}$. An uncirculated Golden Eagle coin has a mass of $13.714\,\text{g}$.

**How much mass has Darragh's coin lost?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{g}$
hints: Uncirculated mass $-$ current mass $=$ lost mass.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&&\\
&1&3.&7&1&4\\
-&1&3.&5&5&1\\
\hline
&&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
Darragh's coin has lost $0.163\,\text{g}$ of mass.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mafalda has a save percentage of $0.738$ so far this hockey season. Last season, her save percentage was $0.585$.

**How much has Mafalda's save percentage improved?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: Current season's save percentage $-$ last season's save percentage $=$ amount of improvement.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&\\
&0.&7&3&8\\
-&0.&5&8&5\\
\hline
&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cayden has several screws on a scale, and the scale reads $80.955\,\text{g}$. Cayden adds $1$ more screw, and the scale reads $84.81\,\text{g}$. 

**What is the mass of the last screw Cayden adds?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]  $\text{g}$
hints: New mass $-$ previous mass $=$ mass of the screw.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&&\\
&8&4.&8&1&0\\
-&8&0.&9&5&5\\
\hline
&&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.

assessment_items.question_content:
Orla and Eduardo each looked at a strand of their hair under a microscope and measured the diameter. Orla's strand was $0.005\,\text{cm}$ in diameter, and Eduardo's strand was $0.012\,\text{cm}$ in diameter.

**How much greater was the diameter of Eduardo's hair?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]  $\text{cm}$
hints: Eduardo's diameter $-$ Orla's diameter $=$ difference in diameter.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&\\
&0.&0&1&2\\
-&0.&0&0&5\\
\hline
&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
The diameter of Eduardo's hair was $0.007\,\text{cm}$ greater than the diameter of Orla's hair.

assessment_items.question_content:
At full price, Melvin would pay $1.679$ dollars per liter of gasoline. Using his loyalty card, Melvin only pays $1.439$ dollars per liter.

**How much less does Melvin pay per liter using his loyalty card?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]  dollars
hints: Full price $-$ price with card $=$ difference in price.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&&\\
&1.&6&7&9\\
-&1.&4&3&9\\
\hline
&.&&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
Melvin pays $0.24$ dollars less per liter with his loyalty card.

assessment_items.question_content:
During a recent rainstorm, $8$ centimeters of rain fell in Dubaku's hometown, and $2.36$ centimeters of rain fell in Elliot's hometown. During the same storm, $15.19$ centimeters of snow fell in Alperen's hometown. 

 **How much more rain fell in Dubaku's town than in Elliot's town?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints: Rain in Dubaku's town $-$ rain in Elliot's town $=$ difference in amount of rain.

$\begin{matrix}
&&&\\
&8.&0&0\\
-&2.&3&6\\
\hline
&.&&\\
\end{matrix}$

We line up the digits by place value, using the decimal point to help us.
There were $5.64$ more centimeters of rain in Dubaku's town than in Elliot's town.
